title: MongoDB has no cheap server-side join
description: $lookup is a per-document left-outer join that behaves like a nested loop, so it is not a substitute for relational joins.
body: Reference for cross-collection queries. $lookup performs a left-outer join by running a lookup against the foreign collection for each input document, so without an index on the joined field it degrades like a nested-loop join. Use when a combined view genuinely spans two collections. Do not lean on $lookup as a routine join the way SQL does; if data is almost always read together, the model should embed it instead. Validation signals include explain showing the $lookup's foreign-field query served by an IXSCAN and a bounded executionTimeMillis. Risk boundary: a $lookup across large collections without an indexed foreign field can scan the foreign collection per row, so confirm the join key is indexed or reconsider the model.
